channel.title: Azure News Feed
channel.description: Aggregated daily news from Azure blogs

item.title: WSL 3 Request MCT AI-Engineer Associate
item.description: Hello,I have an Asus Snapdragon X2E94100, 48gb unified memory 80 TOPS NPU. I have Join the release Preview Insider Program. I am a MCT certified AI-Engineer looking to get on the list for the 'WSL 3' to enable the NPU for local model development using 'MS AI Foundry'. At a very low level I am...
item.dc:creator: CodeChaser

item.title: one drive mapped network drive Win 10
item.description: I want to use my onedrive CID number to connect to a mapped network drive in windows explorer. But, as soon as I type my microsoft account credentials at the login prompt, it doesn't log in but just loops back to the login screen window. What should I do ? Has microsoft stopped allowing onedrive...
item.dc:creator: anmac1789

item.title: How we made GitHub Copilot CLI more selective about delegation
item.description: Better orchestration, fewer handoffs, faster progress, without a single new knob. The post How we made GitHub Copilot CLI more selective about delegation appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Dylan Birtolo

item.title: Copilot code review: New configurations and controls
item.description: With new organization runner controls, Copilot content exclusion support, and the removal of the character limit on repository custom instructions, Copilot code review is now easier to tailor to your… The post Copilot code review: New configurations and controls appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Feature Request: Add native Mute/Unmute controls to Teams interface on Android Auto
item.description: The current Microsoft Teams integration for Android Auto lacks basic in-call management. When participating in an active Teams meeting or VoIP call while driving, there is no option on the vehicle's display to mute or unmute the microphone. Because Teams routes calls through the native dialer...
item.dc:creator: lordneeko

item.title: GitHub Actions: Minimum version enforcement timeline for self-hosted runners
item.description: GitHub Actions is resuming enforcement of version requirements for self-hosted runners on github.com and GitHub Enterprise Cloud with Data Residency. This change is part of a broader effort to rebuild… The post GitHub Actions: Minimum version enforcement timeline for self-hosted runners appeared...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: ArchAngel: Skilling the next developer generation for the Agentic transformation.
item.description: AI is transforming the SDLC at speed but there's a quieter question following close behind. If the code is being written for your junior developers, when do they learn the skills to become senior ones and how can they use these tools without losing understanding of what they've made? Most tools try...
item.dc:creator: RohitMadhavKrishnan

item.title: Microsoft to Delete Unlicensed OneDrive for Business Accounts
item.description: Microsoft will delete unlicensed OneDrive for Business accounts that aren’t paid for (to be archived) after July 2026. Up to now, it’s been possible to leave unpaid-for accounts linger in Microsoft 365 archive until retention policies and holds expire. Now, tenants must decide which accounts they...
item.dc:creator: TonyRedmond

item.title: The AI Blind Spot in Unified Communications: Are Organizations Ready for What's Coming?
item.description: We are in the middle of a quiet transformation. AI has moved from the periphery of enterprise technology into the very core of how people communicate, collaborate, and make decisions. Microsoft Copilot sits inside Teams. AI-driven summarization tools are embedded in Zoom. Intelligent assistants now...
item.dc:creator: AmitMohanSaklani90

item.title: CanReset value flipping on cloud only devices
item.description: Hello, I have a problem with cloud only Windows 11 devices configured with passwordless policy. I have noticed that when you run dsregcmd /status command, CanReset value under User State is flipping between "No" and "DestructiveAndNonDestructive". When it's latter, everything works fine, users can...
item.dc:creator: Mariusz_80

item.title: Orchestration Patterns for Multi-Agent Systems: Performance and Trade-offs
item.description: Real-world lessons from evolving a production chatbot into a coordinator-based multi-agent architecture, including performance trade-offs for enterprise-scale agent reuse. The post Orchestration Patterns for Multi-Agent Systems: Performance and Trade-offs appeared first on ISE Developer Blog.
item.dc:creator: Lily Jia

item.title: Troubleshooting ML Model Loading, GPU Issues, and Memory Pressure in Azure Container Apps
item.description: Introduction Deploying an AI application to Azure Container Apps is fundamentally different from deploying a web API. When you containerize a Django REST API, the application starts in a few seconds, the memory footprint is predictable, and the CPU usage scales linearly with requests. When you...
item.dc:creator: BhaktiRath95

item.title: Struggling to get managers to actually use 1:1 meeting agendas in Teams
item.description: We've been trying to get our managers to run structured 1:1s with their direct reports using Teams. Right now they just hop on a call with no agenda and wing it. HR wants there to be a documented agenda, talking points from both sides, and some kind of record of what was discussed. We tried using...
item.dc:creator: EmmaHastings

item.title: GitHub Enterprise Server 3.21 is now generally available
item.description: GitHub Enterprise Server (GHES) 3.21 enhances deployment efficiency, monitoring capabilities, code security, and policy management. Here are a few highlights in the 3.21 release: Organization custom properties are now generally… The post GitHub Enterprise Server 3.21 is now generally available...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: June Patches for Azure DevOps Server
item.description: We are releasing new patches for our self‑hosted product, Azure DevOps Server. We strongly recommend that all customers stay up to date with the latest, most secure version of Azure DevOps Server. The most recent release, Azure DevOps Server, is available on the download page. The following...
item.dc:creator: Gloridel Morales

item.title: GitHub availability report: May 2026
item.description: In May, we experienced nine incidents that resulted in degraded performance across GitHub services. The post GitHub availability report: May 2026 appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Natalie Guevara

item.title: Making Mac OneDrive Faster and More Reliable
item.description: OneDrive on macOS is getting a major update to how it syncs! The Native Sync Engine is rolling out to Insiders today with version 26.098. It's up to 2x faster on initial sync and on-disk state changes, uses less CPU, memory, and battery, and removes the hidden cache folder that's been the source of...
item.dc:creator: Jack_Nichols

item.title: Migrating EWS notifications to Microsoft Graph
item.description: Migrating from the Exchange Web Services (EWS) notification framework, which supports push, pull, and streaming notification types — to the Microsoft Graph subscription model represents a fundamental transition toward a unified, stateless, and event-driven framework. The post Migrating EWS...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft Graph team

item.title: Right‑sizing Azure Savings Plans, one hour at a time
item.description: Most of us have, at some point, sized a commitment the same way we order food for a team offsite: estimate, round up, and hope nobody complains. It works for pizza. It is a less robust strategy for a three‑year Savings Plan. Azure Cost Management already produces commitment recommendations for you,...
item.dc:creator: Dirk_Brinkmann

item.title: A deep dive into Azure Bastion session recording
item.description: If you’ve worked with Azure Bastion’s Premium SKU, you’ve likely encountered one of its most powerful security features: graphical session recording. Capturing RDP and SSH sessions end to end strengthens compliance, supports forensic investigations, and improves operational accountability. How you...
item.dc:creator: juquint

item.title: Bot-created pull requests can run workflows if approved
item.description: Pull requests created by the github-actions[bot] are now able to run your CI/CD workflows with user approval. Requiring approval is a security measure to ensure generated code does not automatically… The post Bot-created pull requests can run workflows if approved appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: A new way to create visuals on Real-Time Dashboards – (Preview)
item.description: Discover a new way to build visuals in Real-Time Dashboards. The redesigned tile editing experience in Real-Time Dashboards brings AI-assisted authoring, a larger preview area, and more flexible workflows to help you create and refine dashboard visuals faster.
item.dc:creator: MichalBar

item.title: Modern VM monitoring, powered by OpenTelemetry
item.description: At Build 2026, we're announcing the general availability of OpenTelemetry (OTel) Guest OS metrics for Azure VMs and Arc-enabled Servers. OTel provides a standards-based foundation for VM monitoring with consistent metrics across Windows and Linux, richer Guest OS and per-process visibility, and...
item.dc:creator: viviandiec

item.title: What to Do When You Hit Capacity in Azure Databricks: Engage, Mitigate, Plan!
item.description: Microsoft's Cloud Architects: Paul Singh PaulSingh​, Eduardo Dos Santos eduardomdossantos​, Chris Walk cwalk​, Peter Lo PeterLo​, Tim Orentlikher tim_orentlikher​, Ajmal Hossain ajmalhossain​, Chris Haynes Chris_Haynes​, and Rafia Aqil Rafia_Aqil​ Start Here: Engage Microsoft Capacity constraints...
item.dc:creator: Rafia_Aqil

item.title: AI usage report updates
item.description: Your AI usage reports now reflect GitHub AI Credits usage in the standard report fields. To monitor AI credit usage going forward, use quantity for AI credit quantity and gross_amount… The post AI usage report updates appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Azure Monitor SLIs now Generally Available
item.description: Azure Monitor SLIs are now generally available Service Level Indicators (SLIs) and Service Level Objectives (SLOs) in Azure Monitor are now generally available. Teams can now measure reliability based on customer experience, not just infrastructure signals. SLI: A quantitative measure of how well...
item.dc:creator: Sokuma

item.title: Azure Monitor Metrics Export Generally Available
item.description: Today, we’re excited to announce the general availability of Azure Monitor Metrics Export using data collection rules (DCRs). A scalable, flexible way to continuously export platform metrics with dimensional fidelity, lower latency, and more control over what you send downstream. Azure Monitor...
item.dc:creator: Sokuma

item.title: Time Series Visualization in Real-Time Dashboard (Preview)
item.description: Time series analysis is at the heart of understanding how data behaves over time. Whether you’re tracking sensor readings, monitoring system performance, or analyzing business trends, the ability to visualize and explore time-based data is essential for making informed decisions.
item.dc:creator: MichalBar

item.title: Copilot CLI: Configure everything from one place with /settings
item.description: GitHub Copilot CLI now has a unified, schema-driven home for configuration. The new /settings slash command combines the scattered commands like /theme, /streamer-mode, and /experimental with options that previously required… The post Copilot CLI: Configure everything from one place with /settings...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: New runner images in public preview
item.description: Two new GitHub-hosted runner images for GitHub Actions are now available in public preview for all users, giving you early access to test your workflows on the latest platforms before… The post New runner images in public preview appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Erro 0x0800f0843
item.description: Boa tarde, fui baixar a atualização 26220.8575 e aparece esse erro, algum pode ria me ajudar.
item.dc:creator: Pa-1970

item.title: GitHub Agentic Workflows is now in public preview
item.description: GitHub Agentic Workflows is now in public preview. With agentic workflows, you can automate reasoning-based tasks like issue triage, CI failure analysis, and documentation updates by leveraging coding agents inside… The post GitHub Agentic Workflows is now in public preview appeared first on The...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Making secret scanning more trustworthy: Reducing false positives at scale
item.description: Alerts are more trustworthy and actionable when noise is reduced. See how we improved the verification step with context-aware LLM reasoning. The post Making secret scanning more trustworthy: Reducing false positives at scale appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Natalie Guevara

item.title: Live refresh for Real-Time Dashboards (Generally Available)
item.description: Real-Time Dashboards in Microsoft Fabric help you monitor live data and react to changes as they happen. Today, we're announcing a new feature that automatically refreshes your dashboard visuals when new data is ingested into the dashboard’s underlying data sources.
item.dc:creator: MichalBar

item.title: Reduce unnecessary internet exposure with Microsoft Defender
item.description: In today’s threat landscape, internet exposure, i.e. devices that allow inbound connectivity from the public internet, continues to be a major vector for initial access and compromise. Devices that are exposed to the public internet can significantly increase an organization’s attack surface,...
item.dc:creator: hadarshindler

item.title: Agentic workflows no longer need a personal access token
item.description: You can now use GitHub Agentic Workflows with GitHub Actions’s built-in GITHUB_TOKEN. This means that you no longer need to create and store a personal access token (PAT), eliminating the… The post Agentic workflows no longer need a personal access token appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Copilot Autofix for GitHub Advanced Security for Azure DevOps
item.description: Over the last few years, we’ve encouraged customers to move their repositories from Azure Repos to GitHub, where the newest AI-powered and agentic development experiences land first. Migrating isn’t equally simple for everyone. A move to GitHub can range from straightforward to a multi-year...
item.dc:creator: Laura Jiang

item.title: Azure Functions at Build 2026 Update
item.description: Azure Functions took another big leap at Build 2026. It is now the best programming model for event-driven apps and agents, on the best infrastructure to write secure code that scales. The headline features: serverless agents, connectors to M365, Teams, and more, Go, MCP, and Durable Tasks....
item.dc:creator: nzthiago

item.title: Review pull requests without leaving Visual Studio
item.description: Pull request integration in Visual Studio has been one of the most requested Git features. Developers have been asking for a way to open a PR, inspect the changes, discuss feedback, and finish the review without switching to the browser. The feedback on that request has played a big role in shaping...
item.dc:creator: Leah Tran

item.title: Beyond markdown: Shareable sites with Rayfin (Preview)
item.description: If you work alongside AI tools, your screen is probably full of markdown files. They’re fast to write, easy for an agent to read, and great for keeping a record. Then you try to share one. That’s where the format breaks. You paste it into a chat and the formatting collapses. You email the file and...
item.dc:creator: analyticanna

item.title: Real-Time Simulation with Ansys Discovery on Azure AMD Radeon V710 GPUs
item.description: Overview of Synopsys-Ansys Discovery Ansys Discovery is an interactive engineering application that unifies 3D geometry modeling, real‑time simulation, and visual analysis into a single, immersive workspace. It is purpose‑built to accelerate early‑stage engineering exploration while maintaining...
item.dc:creator: Sunita_AZ0708

item.title: Microsoft Fabric to Azure Databricks: Another Better Together Story!
item.description: Author's: Oscar Alvarado oscaralvarado​ and Rafia Aqil Rafia_Aqil​ Note: This article describes a solution idea. Your cloud architect can use this guidance to help visualize the major components for a typical implementation. Use this article as a starting point to design a well-architected solution...
item.dc:creator: Rafia_Aqil

item.title: Dynamic Connection Properties in Azure Logic Apps Standard
item.description: Switching Built-in Connections at Runtime The Problem Imagine your organization has a single Logic App workflow used by multiple teams. Each team has its own SFTP server, database, or service bus namespace. Traditionally, you'd need separate workflows or hardcode a single connection — neither...
item.dc:creator: ShubhamSachdeva99

item.title: Debug App Startup Faster on Azure App Service for Linux with Startup Logs
item.description: When an app fails to start on Azure App Service for Linux, one of the first things you need is visibility into what happened during startup. This can include container initialization, runtime setup, startup command execution, application output, and warmup probe results. To make this easier, we...
item.dc:creator: TulikaC

item.title: Released: June 2026 Exchange Server Security Updates
item.description: Microsoft has released Security Updates (SUs) for vulnerabilities found in: Exchange Server Subscription Edition (SE) Exchange Server 2019 Exchange Server 2016 SUs are available for the following specific versions of Exchange Server: Exchange SE RTM Exchange Server 2019 CU14 and CU15 (to access,...
item.dc:creator: The_Exchange_Team

item.title: Communities tab in Teams
item.description: Hi there I've recently had the Communities tab pop up in my Teams alongside the Teams and Channels tab. No one else in my organisation can see this yet and we aren't sure why. I know it's being rolled out on a timeline but I was also wondering if it might be because I'm the only one in the org who...
item.dc:creator: gp4

item.title: Primer: Finding Sensitivity Labels with PowerShell
item.description: Three cmdlets exist to fetch sensitivity labels. One is in the Exchange Online module; the others are powered by Graph APIs. What are the differences between each method and how can you make sure that the set of sensitivity labels fetched by PowerShell is the full set of available labels? These and...
item.dc:creator: TonyRedmond

item.title: Preview update turned full win11 license into an evaluation
item.description: Hello community, something weird here, a Lenovo laptop bought new with a full win 11 license (not sure 100% if it was win11 or 10 upgraded to 11)after one of the latest preview updates turned to an evaluation copy with timed expiration.Edition Windows 11 Pro Insider PreviewVersion 25H2Installed on...
item.dc:creator: Deskart

item.title: Performance Tuning Cold Starts, Scaling Delays, and Startup Latency in Azure Container Apps
item.description: Introduction There is a particular kind of frustration that comes not when your application fails to start, but when it starts too slowly. The container is running, the health probes pass, your monitoring shows green — and yet every few minutes a user somewhere in the world hits a request that...
item.dc:creator: BhaktiRath95

item.title: Agents League Hackathon 2026 – Enterprise Agents
item.description: From declarative agents for Microsoft 365 Copilot to fully autonomous multi-agent systems orchestrating complex workflows, developers now have the power to redefine how work gets done. If you’re ready to push those boundaries, Agents League Hackathon 2026 is your stage. What is Agents League?...
item.dc:creator: Paolo Pialorsi
